schemes.brief: Saksham is a Scholarship Scheme by the Ministry of Education, implemented by AICTE, aimed at providing encouragement and support to specially-abled children to pursue technical education. This is an attempt to give every young specially-abled student the opportunity to study further.

schemes.name: Biotechnology Career Advancement And Re-orientation (BioCARe) Programme For Women Scientists
schemes.brief: Biotechnology Career Advancement and Re-orientation (BioCARe) Programme, a special scheme of the Department of Biotechnology (DBT), Ministry of Science & Technology, the government of India aims to enhance the participation of women scientists in India towards research in Biotechnology.

schemes.name: Internship Program At The Indian Patent Office
schemes.brief: The Internship Program by the Indian Patent Office recognizes the abundance of creative and innovative energies that flow in India, and the need to tap into and channelize these energies. The program aims to work towards the fulfilment of the objectives of the National IPR Policy.

schemes.name: SAHAKAR MITRA: NCDC’s Scheme on Internship Programme
schemes.ministry: Ministry Of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare
schemes.brief: The programme aims at providing an opportunity to professional graduates to get experience in areas of functioning of NCDC and related aspects of cooperatives.

schemes.name: Rajbhasha Gaurav Puraskar Scheme for writing original book in Hindi
schemes.brief: The scheme aims to encourage original and translated book writing in Hindi. Through this scheme, monetary awards, certificates, and mementos are provided to Indian authors.

schemes.name: Visvesvaraya PhD Scheme for Electronics and IT: Post-Doctoral Fellowships (PDF)
schemes.ministry: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology
schemes.brief: This fellowship is meant for those who have a proven track record as evident from their research publications & recognitions. Support is provided for 225 Post-Doctoral Fellowships for candidates for a period of 1 year, to encourage specialized research in emerging area.

schemes.name: National Agriculture Market
schemes.ministry: Ministry Of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare
schemes.brief: Launched on 14th April 2016, e-NAM is a pan-India electronic trading platform that links existing mandis to create a unified online market for agricultural commodities, promoting “One Nation One Market” and enabling transparent, efficient, and nationwide agri-trade.

schemes.name: Research and Development Programme in Water Sector
schemes.ministry: Ministry Of Water Resources,River Development & Ganga Rejuvenation
schemes.brief: The scheme aims to solve water sector problems through effective research and development. It provides Grants-in-Aid to academicians, universities, laboratories, government departments, and Non-Governmental Organizations to fund basic, applied, and action research projects.

schemes.name: Financial Support Scheme
schemes.brief: The scheme aims to provide financial assistance to financially weak students of the University of Delhi to help them overcome financial challenges and continue their education without hardship.

schemes.name: Assistance to Voluntary Organisations for Promotion of the Official Languages in the Field of Law
schemes.brief: The scheme aims to propagate and develop literature in the field of law in Hindi and other regional languages. It provides financial grants to voluntary organisations for publishing legal books, commentaries, and journals.

schemes.name: Innovation In Science Pursuit For Inspired Research (INSPIRE) - Faculty Fellowship
schemes.brief: The "INSPIRE FACULTY FELLOWSHIP" component provides opportunities to 100 persons every year with Ph.D. qualifications in the age group of 27-32 years for 5 years to carry out research in both basic and applied science areas including engineering, medicine, agriculture, and veterinary sciences.

schemes.name: Ecomark Scheme of India (ECOMARK)
schemes.ministry: Ministry Of Environment,forests and climate change
schemes.brief: The scheme aims to encourage the demand for environment-friendly products that cause lesser adverse impacts on the environment. It provides an Ecomark label for products meeting specified environmental criteria to eligible producers, manufacturers, importers, and exporters.

schemes.name: Scheme of Cash Incentive to Medal Winners in International Sports Events and their Coaches
schemes.brief: The objective of the scheme is to reward outstanding sportspersons for their achievements at the global level. It aims to simplify eligibility, improve the award process, motivate athletes to achieve higher, and inspire young people to take up sports.

schemes.name: Mission Vatsalya (Child Protection Services and Child Welfare Services)
schemes.ministry: Ministry of Women and Child Development
schemes.brief: This scheme aims to ensure, sustain, and strengthen child protection at the family and community levels, promote family-based non-institutional care, and provide financial support to children in need.

schemes.name: PRL's Vikram Sarabhai Postdoctoral Fellowship (VISHWAS)
schemes.brief: The Vikram Sarabhai Postdoctoral Fellowship (VISHWAS) program is an initiative by the Physical Research Laboratory (PRL) to honor the legacy of Prof. Vikram Sarabhai by supporting young doctorates to pursue groundbreaking research in fundamental science with societal impact.

schemes.name: NSAP - Indira Gandhi National Old Age Pension Scheme
schemes.brief: "Indira Gandhi National Old Age Pension Scheme" is one of the five sub-schemes of the National Social Assistance Programme. Under IGNOAPS, citizens living Below Poverty Line and 60 years or above in age are eligible to apply. A monthly pension of ₹ 200 up to 79 years and ₹ 500 thereafter.

schemes.name: Buti Foundation Award
schemes.brief: The Buti Foundation Award, established in 2007 by the Buti Foundation, New Delhi, is a biennial award recognizing innovative and outstanding research in the field of Plasma Science & Technology. The award aims to honor a Scientist/Engineer under the age of 45 years for their significant contribution

schemes.name: AFFDF-Financial Assistance For Treatment Of Serious Diseases To Non Pensioner Ex-Servicemen (All Ranks)/Widows
schemes.brief: The scheme aims to provide financial assistance to non-pensioner Ex-Servicemen of all ranks and widows for meeting medical expenses incurred on the treatment of approved serious diseases, including cancer, renal failure, knee replacement, and heart surgery.

schemes.name: ICAR Emeritus Scientist Scheme
schemes.ministry: Ministry Of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare
schemes.brief: The scheme aims to utilize the expertise of outstanding superannuated scientists of the National Agricultural Research System by supporting nationally important research, teaching, human resource development, and extension activities through honorarium and research grants.

schemes.name: ICAR Emeritus Professor
schemes.ministry: Ministry Of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare
schemes.brief: The ICAR floated the Scheme of Emeritus Professor in order to provide an opportunity to the superannuated teachers/ Scientists of universities, colleges and institutions under ICAR-AU System.

schemes.name: Skill Loan Scheme
schemes.ministry: Ministry Of Skill Development And Entrepreneurship
schemes.brief: The scheme aims to support youth by providing access to institutional credit for skill training courses. Through this scheme, collateral-free loans and credit guarantee support are provided to eligible borrowers for undertaking skill training programmes.

schemes.name: Central Sector Scheme for Rehabilitation of Bonded Labourer
schemes.brief: The scheme addresses eradicating bonded labour in India. It introduces varying rehabilitation assistance amounts based on beneficiary categories, such as adults, children, women, and extreme cases, with provisions for annuity schemes and Direct Benefit Transfer.

schemes.name: RMEWF-Financial Assistance For Medical Treatment Of Ex-Servicemen
schemes.brief: The aim of this aid gratis is to provide financial assistance to non-pensioner ESM or their widow to meet routine medical expenses during the year.

schemes.name: NIDHI – Seed Support Program (SSP)
schemes.brief: The key objective of the NIDHI- Seed Support Program (NIDHI-SSP) is to ensure availability of the seed support through incubators for providing timely support to potential startups, thereby enabling them to take their ventures to the next level and facilitate their success in the marketplace.

schemes.name: Integrated Scheme for Export Promotion & Quality Improvement in Spices and Research and Development of Cardamom: Post-Harvest Improvement: Pepper thresher (nos)
schemes.brief: To motivate the pepper growers to produce good quality pepper for export by promoting installation of pepper threshers for hygienic separation of pepper berries from the spikes.

schemes.name: Integrated Scheme for Export Promotion & Quality Improvement in Spices and Research and Development of Cardamom: Post-Harvest Improvement: ICCD for Small Cardamom (nos) (Improved Cardamom Curing Devices)
schemes.brief: The scheme aims to encourage cardamom growers to adopt Improved Cardamom Curing Devices (ICCD) for drying small cardamom, thereby improving the quality of the produce and making it more suitable for export.

schemes.name: Self Employment Scheme For Rehabilitation Of Manual Scavengers
schemes.ministry: Ministry Of Social Justice and Empowerment
schemes.brief: The Self Employment Scheme for Rehabilitation of Manual Scavengers (SRMS) was introduced in January 2017 by the Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment, to assist the identified manual scavengers and their dependants in rehabilitation in alternative occupations.

schemes.name: Rainfed Area Development
schemes.ministry: Ministry Of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare
schemes.brief: Rainfed Area Development (RAD) scheme was launched in 2014-15 to mainstream development of rainfed areas in a sustainable manner. It adopts an area-based approach, focuses on Integrated Farming System (IFS) for enhancing productivity and minimizing risks associated with climatic variability.

schemes.name: Credit Linked Subsidy Scheme for Middle Income Group
schemes.brief: Under Credit Linked Subsidy Scheme, beneficiaries of Middle-Income Group (MIG) can seek housing loans from Banks, Housing Finance Companies and other such institutions for acquisition/ construction of house (including re-purchase).

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Janjatiya Vikas Mission (PMJVM)
schemes.brief: The scheme aims to promote livelihood generation and income enhancement of tribal communities through value addition, processing, and marketing of tribal products.

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Awaas Yojana - Gramin
schemes.brief: Launch on 1st April 2016, Pradhan Mantri Awaas Yojana Gramin (PMAY-G) is centre's flagship mission by the Ministry of Rural Development (MoRD), implemented by the Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs (MoHUA). PMAY-G addresses the rural housing shortage and bridges the housing deficit in rural areas

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Garib Kalyan Anna Yojana
schemes.brief: The scheme aims to ensure food security for the poor. Through this scheme, free foodgrains are provided to eligible ration card holders under the National Food Security Act, 2013.

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Ujjwala Yojana
schemes.ministry: Ministry Of Petroleum and Natural Gas
schemes.brief: A scheme by Ministry of Petroleum and Natural Gas for Providing free LPG connections to women belonging to the Below Poverty Line (BPL) households.

schemes.name: Relief and Rehabilitation Assistance to Sri Lankan Refugees in the Refugee Camps
schemes.brief: The scheme provides relief materials, which among other things include Feeding Charges, Subsidized Rice, Clothing Materials, Utensils, Transportation Charges (on arrival), Porterage and Transport (on repatriation), Cash Doles, Infrastructure Facilities (at camps), Electricity, Drinking Water, etc.

schemes.name: Ayushman Bharat - Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana
schemes.brief: AB PM-JAY is a health insurance scheme for low-income families in rural and urban areas. The scheme aims to provide affordable healthcare facilities to the Poor. It is the largest health assurance scheme in the world which aims at providing a health cover of ₹5,00,000/- per family per year.

schemes.name: National Family Benefit Scheme
schemes.brief: A family welfare scheme under the umbrella scheme ‘National Social Assistance Programme’ (NSAP) by MoRD. In this scheme, financial assistance is provided to the bereaved households in case of the death of the primary breadwinner irrespective of the cause of death.

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana - Urban
schemes.brief: Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana - Urban (PMAY-U) Scheme was launched on 25th June 2015 by the Hon’ble Prime Minister to provide all-weather pucca houses to all eligible urban households across the nation with the vision of ‘Housing for All’.

schemes.name: Scheme for Containing Population Decline of Small Minority Communities
schemes.brief: The objective of the scheme is to reverse the declining trend of Parsi population by adopting a scientific protocol and structured interventions, stabilize their population and increase the population of Parsis in India.

schemes.name: Physical Research Laboratory Award
schemes.brief: The PRL Award, instituted by the late Prof. Devendra Lal, former Director of the Physical Research Laboratory (PRL), is awarded biennially to recognize outstanding Indian scientists for original work in Earth & Planetary systems, including areas like Oceanography, Atmospheric Sciences & Meteorology.

schemes.name: Loan Based Schemes For Safai Karamchari - General Term Loan (GTL)
schemes.ministry: Ministry Of Social Justice and Empowerment
schemes.brief: A general term loan scheme by the Ministry of Social justice and Empowerment for Safai Karamchari, Manual Scavengers, and their dependants aged 18 years and above.

schemes.name: Surakshit Matritva Aashwasan Yojana
schemes.brief: The scheme aims to provide assured, dignified, and completely free healthcare services to all pregnant women, mothers, and newborns at public health facilities.

schemes.name: Central Assistance For One Time Settlement Of Displaced Families From PoK And Chhamb Under Prime Minister's Development Package
schemes.brief: The scheme aims to provide supplementary financial assistance to displaced families for assured and sustained earnings. Through this scheme, one-time financial assistance of ₹5,50,000/- per family is provided to eligible displaced families of PoK and Chhamb settled in Jammu & Kashmir.

schemes.name: Scheme for Supportive Money to the Parents of Transgender Children
schemes.ministry: Ministry Of Social Justice and Empowerment
schemes.brief: The "Scheme for Supportive Money to the Parents of Transgender Children" aims to provide financial assistance of ₹1,000/- to the parents of transgender children. Transgender persons face stigma and discrimination in almost every sphere of their life including within the group, health facilities.

schemes.name: Swachh Bharat Mission (Grameen) Phase II
schemes.brief: A sanitation scheme for Visual cleanliness through Open Defecation Free (ODF) sustainability and effective management of solid and liquid waste in villages.

schemes.name: Swachh Bharat Mission – Urban 2.0
schemes.brief: A sanitation scheme for the construction of Individual Household Latrines (IHHL) for urban households.

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Adi Adarsh Gram Yojana (PMAAGY)
schemes.brief: The scheme aims to develop selected villages with significant Scheduled Tribe population into model villages by providing basic infrastructure and improving socio-economic conditions.

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Kisan Samman Nidhi (PM-KISAN)
schemes.ministry: Ministry Of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare
schemes.brief: With a view to provide income support to all landholding farmers’ families in the country, having cultivable land, the Central Government has implemented a Central Sector Scheme, namely, “Pradhan Mantri Kisan Samman Nidhi (PM-KISAN)”.

schemes.name: Rashtriya Parivar Sahayata Yojana
schemes.ministry: Ministry Of Social Justice and Empowerment
schemes.brief: This scheme is being executed for the family members who are unable to make their livelihood after the death of the primary breadwinner of the family.

schemes.name: Jal Jeevan Mission
schemes.brief: Jal Jeevan Mission, is envisioned to provide safe and adequate drinking water through individual household tap connections by 2024 to all households in rural India.

schemes.name: One Nation One Ration Card
schemes.ministry: Ministry Of Consumer Affairs, Food And Public Distribution
schemes.brief: "One Nation, One Ration Card" is Aadhaar-based national ration card portability scheme to ensure food security for all, including internal migrants within India, under which beneficiaries can purchase subsidized food anywhere in India.

schemes.name: Aam Aadmi Bima Yojana
schemes.brief: The Aam Aadmi Bima Yojana (AABY) is a social security scheme for rural landless households implemented by the Ministry of Labour & Employment, Government of India. The scheme provides insurance coverage to the head of the household or an earning member of the family in the age group of 18 to 59.

schemes.name: PM – Surya Ghar: Muft Bijli Yojana
schemes.ministry: Ministry Of New and Renewable Energy
schemes.brief: "PM Surya Ghar: Muft Bijli Yojana" is a government scheme that aims to provide free electricity to households in India.

schemes.name: Prime Minister’s Scholarship Scheme for the Wards of States/UTs Police Personnel
schemes.brief: The scheme aims to support higher education for dependent wards of States/UTs Police personnel martyred during Terror/Naxal attacks. Through this scheme, financial assistance for girls and boys per year is provided.

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Mudra Yojana
schemes.brief: Pradhan Mantri Mudra Yojana (PMMY) is  a flagship scheme of Government of India. It facilitates micro credit/Loan upto Rs. 20 lakhs to income generating micro enterprises engaged in the non farm sector in manufacturing, processing, trading or service sector including activities allied to agriculture

schemes.name: Atal Mission For Rejuvenation And Urban Transformation
schemes.brief: Providing basic services (e.g. water supply, sewerage, urban transport) to households and building amenities in cities which will improve the quality of life for all, especially the poor and the disadvantaged is a national priority.

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Adarsh Gram Yojana
schemes.ministry: Ministry Of Social Justice and Empowerment
schemes.brief: With a view to enabling an area-based development approach, a new scheme Pradhan Mantri Adarsh Gram Yojana (PMAGY) was launched during 2009-10. The Scheme aims at the integrated development of Scheduled Castes majority villages.

schemes.name: AICTE - Grant For Augmenting Infrastructure In North Eastern Region
schemes.brief: The scheme "AICTE - Grant for Augmenting Infrastructure in North Eastern Region (GAINER)" was introduced by AICTE to enhance the functional efficiency of the technical institutes located in far-flung areas of North East India, by addressing deficiencies in water, electricity, and internet.

schemes.name: National Handicrafts Development Programme: Infrastructure And Technology Support: EMPORIA
schemes.brief: In the scheme "Infrastructure and Technology Support: EMPORIA" assistance would be provided for the setting up of emporia. These would be set up in commercially viable locations in the implementing agencies' own/rented buildings.

schemes.name: National Awards For Empowerment Of Persons With Disabilities: National Awards For Institutions Engaged In Empowering Persons With Disabilities: Divyangjano Ke Liye Sarvshrestha Placement Agency
schemes.ministry: Ministry Of Social Justice and Empowerment
schemes.brief: The "Divyangjano Ke Liye Sarvshrestha Placement Agency" award is given by DoEPwD to the placement agency of PwDs (excluding Govt./State Govt./local bodies). The ceremony of the National Awards is held at Vigyan Bhawan. The National Awards are conferred by the Hon'ble President / Vice President.

schemes.name: Development Support for Coffee in Traditional Areas: Replantation
schemes.brief: The objective of the scheme "Development Support for Coffee in Traditional Areas: Replantation" is to improve the production and productivity of coffee plantations by encouraging replantation of old/senile and unproductive plantations with high-yielding, disease-tolerant coffee varieties.

schemes.name: Vocational Training in Tribal Areas
schemes.brief: The scheme will be implemented for the benefit of the Scheduled Tribes as well as PTGs and can be taken up anywhere in the country but priority will be given to remote tribal areas, areas inhabited by particularly vulnerable tribes, and areas affected by extremist activities.

schemes.name: AICTE- TECHNICAL UNIVERSITY JOINT TRAINING PROGRAMS FOR TEACHERS
schemes.brief: AICTE- Technical University Joint Training Programs for Teachers has been launched to train faculty members to prepare them for being successful in a knowledge society.

schemes.name: AICTE - YOUTH UNDERTAKING VISIT FOR ACQUIRING KNOWLEDGE (YUVAK): STUDY TOUR OF ATAL TUNNEL, HIMACHAL PRADESH
schemes.brief:
The scheme is intended for providing Financial Grant for conducting a Study Tour of ATAL TUNNEL
by Students / faculty of AICTE approved Institutions in the field of technical education.

schemes.name: AICTE SHORT TERM TRAINING PROGRAMME-SFURTI SCHEME
schemes.brief: Short Term Training Programme-SFURTI Program aims to provide financial assistance from AICTE to the institutions for conducting faculty and student trainings to enable faculty members and students in the field of technical education.

schemes.name: AICTE-Margdarshan scheme
schemes.brief: Margdarshan Initiative has been introduced by All India Council for Technical Education in 2018 for facilitating the technical institutes in order to improve the quality of technical education as a whole through accreditation by providing access to the facilities available in host institute.

schemes.name: AICTE-ISTE INDUCTION/REFRESHER PROGRAMME SCHEME
schemes.brief: AICTE-ISTE Induction/ Refresher Programme provide financial assistance to AICTE approved institutions/universities in conducting training for the teaching faculties working in technical institutions.

schemes.name: Telecom Technology Development Fund
schemes.brief:
The Telecom Technology Development Fund Scheme (hereinafter referred to as ‘Scheme’) has been
notified for domestic companies and institutions involved in technology design, development,
commercialization of telecommunication products and solutions to enable affordable broadband and mobile services.

schemes.name: National Water Awards
schemes.brief: The objective of the National Water Awards is to encourage the stakeholders to adopt a holistic approach toward water resource management in the country as surface water and groundwater play a signiﬁcant role in the water cycle.

schemes.name: Deen Dayal Upadhyay Grameen Kaushalya Yojana
schemes.brief: DDU-GKY focuses on the rural poor youth and its emphasis on sustainable employment through the prominence and incentives given to post-placement tracking, retention, and career progression. DDU-GKY is designed to provide high-quality skill training opportunities to the rural poor.

schemes.name: Technology Development Fund (TDF) Scheme
schemes.brief: Technology Development Fund (TDF) has been established to promote self-reliance in Defence Technology as a part of the 'Make in India' initiative.

schemes.name: Pandit Deendayal Upadhyaya Telecom Excellence Award
schemes.brief: The scheme aims to provide national recognition, financial incentives, and professional encouragement to Indian citizens and institutions contributing significantly to telecom development and its application in sectors such as agriculture, health, education, and commerce.

schemes.name: The Prime Minister’s Awards for Excellence in Public Administration
schemes.ministry: Ministry Of Personnel, Public Grievances And Pensions
schemes.brief: The scheme aims to reward innovation and governance excellence, this scheme provides recognition and a ₹20,00,000 incentive to districts and organizations.

schemes.name: Financial Assistance To Cultural Organizations With National Presence
schemes.brief: The objective of this scheme component is to provide financial assistance to cultural organizations with a national presence to meet the expenditure incurred in conducting cultural programmes and festivals.

schemes.name: Sagar Samman Award- Sagar Samman Award for Excellence
schemes.brief: This scheme aims to recognize high-level maritime contributions by providing recognition to individuals with sustained excellence.

schemes.name: Tagore National Scholarship for Cultural Research
schemes.brief: The Scheme has been introduced to invigorate and revitalize the various institutions under the Ministry of Culture and other identified cultural institutions in the country, by encouraging Scholars/academicians to affiliate themselves with these institutions to work on projects of mutual interest.

schemes.name: Coir Vikas Yojana-Skill Upgradation and Mahila Coir Yojana
schemes.ministry: Ministry Of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises
schemes.brief: The scheme “Skill Upgradation and Mahila Coir Yojana” is a component of “Coir Vikas Yojana” and the scheme was launched by the Ministry of Micro, Small & Medium Enterprises, Government of India.

schemes.name: Coffee Development Programme in North Eastern Region: Quality Upgradation / Certification: Support for Eco-Certification / Organic Certification
schemes.brief: The scheme aims to encourage coffee growers to improve quality for higher returns and improve access to high-value specialty markets through the production of eco-certified coffees. It also encourages small growers to form groups/collectives to take up eco-certification.

schemes.name: Interest Subsidy Eligibility Certificate (ISEC) Scheme
schemes.ministry: Ministry Of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises
schemes.brief: The scheme aims to provide concessional working capital loans at 4% interest to khadi institutions through interest subsidy support.

schemes.name: Production Linked Incentive (PLI) Scheme For National Programme on Advanced Chemistry Cell (ACC) Battery Storage
schemes.brief: The scheme aims to promote giga-scale ACC manufacturing in India. Through this scheme, financial incentives are provided to selected firms to boost domestic battery manufacturing and reduce imports.

schemes.name: Production Linked Incentive (PLI) Scheme for Textiles Part-1
schemes.brief: The scheme aims to promote MMF and technical textile production for global competitiveness and employment generation. Through this scheme, performance-based financial incentives are provided to textile manufacturers.

schemes.name: Incubation Scheme
schemes.ministry: Ministry Of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises
schemes.brief: Incubation Scheme aims to promote and support untapped creativity and to promote adoption of latest technologies in MSMEs that seek the validation of their ideas at the proof-of-concept level.

schemes.name: Bank Guarantee Charges Reimbursement Scheme
schemes.ministry: Ministry Of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises
schemes.brief: Under the Ministry Of Micro, Small & Medium Enterprises, Bank Guarantee Charges Reimbursement Scheme is a sub scheme under National Scheduled Caste and Scheduled Tribe Hub (NSSH) scheme.

schemes.name: Testing Fee Reimbursement Scheme
schemes.ministry: Ministry Of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises
schemes.brief: Testing Fee Reimbursement Scheme for Scheduled Caste / Scheduled Tribe is a sub scheme under National Scheduled Caste and Scheduled Tribe Hub (NSSH) scheme.

schemes.name: Scheme for Setting Up of Centre of Excellence
schemes.brief: The scheme aims to provide strategic funding for collaborative research and development in the chemicals and petrochemicals sector. It provides a grant of up to ₹5,00,00,000/- to existing Government universities and organizations for research equipment, staff, and technology commercialization.

schemes.name: Scheme of Assistance for the Prevention of Alcoholism & Substance (Drugs) Abuse and for Social Defence Services: General Grant-in-Aid Programme for Financial Assistance in the Field of Social Defence
schemes.ministry: Ministry Of Social Justice and Empowerment
schemes.brief: The scheme aims to meet needs falling within the mandate of the Ministry which cannot be met under its regular schemes and support such initiatives of an innovative/pilot nature in the area of welfare and empowerment of the Ministry’s target groups, as cannot be supported under its regular schemes.

schemes.name: Nagar Van Yojana
schemes.ministry: Ministry Of Environment,forests and climate change
schemes.brief: The scheme aims to develop 400 Nagar Vans and 200 Nagar Vatikas to create bio-diverse green spaces in urban areas. The scheme provides a grant of ₹4,00,000/- per hectare to Municipalities, Urban Local Bodies, and Institutions for afforestation and biodiversity conservation.

schemes.name: PRL Junior Research Fellowships
schemes.brief: The Physical Research Laboratory (PRL), a prestigious institution under the Department of Space, Government of India, launched the Junior Research Fellowship (JRF) Program.

schemes.name: Paramparagat Krishi Vikas Yojana
schemes.ministry: Ministry Of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare
schemes.brief: The Paramparagat Krishi Vikas Yojana (PKVY), launched in 2015, is an extended component of Soil Health Management (SHM) under the Centrally Sponsored Scheme (CSS), National Mission on Sustainable Agriculture (NMSA).

schemes.name: Credit Enhancement Guarantee Scheme For The Scheduled Castes
schemes.ministry: Ministry Of Social Justice and Empowerment
schemes.brief: The scheme aims to promote entrepreneurship, financial inclusion, and employment generation among Scheduled Castes. Through this scheme, credit enhancement guarantees are provided to banks and financial institutions financing eligible Scheduled Caste entrepreneurs.

schemes.name: Janshree Bima Yojana for Khadi Artisans
schemes.ministry: Ministry Of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises
schemes.brief: The scheme aims to provide insurance cover to Khadi artisans. Through this scheme, group insurance benefits are provided to Khadi artisans (spinners and weavers).

schemes.name: Scheme for Plantation Development (New planting & Replanting) under Medium Term Framework Plan (MTFP) in Traditional Areas (Kerala and Tamil Nadu), Non-Traditional and North Eastern Region
schemes.brief: The Scheme is intended to increase the production of natural rubber in India by accelerating new planting and replanting of rubber on scientific lines. Accelerated new planting/replanting would be achieved by giving proper technical guidance and financial assistance to growers.

schemes.name: PRISM (Promoting Innovations in Individuals, Start-ups and MSMEs): Category-1: Proof of Concept/ Prototypes/ Models
schemes.brief: The scheme aims to support individual innovators which will enable them to achieve the agenda of inclusive development - one of the thrust areas of the XIIth five-year plan (2012-2017). The proposals shall preferably be considered in the following focus sectors: Green technology, Clean energy, etc.

schemes.name: Innovations for Defence Excellence
schemes.brief: Aimed at creation of an ecosystem to foster innovation and technology development in Defence and Aerospace by engaging Industries (incl MSMEs, start-ups, individual innovators R&D institutes, academia) and provide them grants and other supports to carry out R&D innovation for adoption in D&A Sector

schemes.name: Institutions of Eminence scheme
schemes.brief: Institutions of Eminence scheme has been launched in order to empower Higher Educational Institutions and to help them become world class teaching and research institutions.

schemes.name: Special Credit Linked Capital Subsidy Scheme (SCLCSS) for Scheduled Caste/Scheduled Tribe
schemes.ministry: Ministry Of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises
schemes.brief: Special Credit Linked Capital Subsidy Scheme (SCLCSS) for Scheduled Caste / Scheduled scheme aims to facilitate capital subsidy to SC/ST MSEs for purchasing of plant & machinery/equipment through institutional finance (bank loan).

schemes.name: AICTE-SHORT TERM TRAINING PROGRAMME (STTP) (UT-J&K,LADAKH AND NER)
schemes.brief: AICTE-Short Term Training Program (STTP) intends to conduct faculty training through financial assistance from AICTE to enable faculty members in the field of technical education.

schemes.name: AICTE- Technical University Joint Training Programs for Librarians
schemes.brief: AICTE- Technical University Joint Training Programs for Librarians, aims to provide financial assistance in conducting training to librarians to prepare them for being successful and effective in a knowledge society.

schemes.name: HRIDAY: Heritage City Development & Augmentation Yojana
schemes.brief: Launched on 21st January 2015, the scheme "National Heritage City Development and Augmentation Yojana", also known as "HRIDAY" is a central sector scheme by the Ministry of Tourism. The scheme was introduced to bring together urban planning, economic growth, and heritage conservation.

schemes.name: Education Loan Scheme (NSFDC)
schemes.ministry: Ministry Of Social Justice and Empowerment
schemes.brief: The scheme implemented by the National Scheduled Castes Finance & Development Corporation, Ministry of Social Justice & Empowerment, Govt. of India. Under this Scheme, loan is provided for pursuing full-time Professional/Technical courses in recognized institutions as one time assistance.

schemes.name: National Livestock Mission: Establishment of Entrepreneurship for Breed Development of Rural Poultry
schemes.ministry: Ministry of Fisheries,Animal Husbandry and Dairying
schemes.brief: The scheme aims to promote entrepreneurship in rural poultry farming. Through this scheme, a 50% capital subsidy is provided for establishing Parent Farms, Rural Hatcheries, and Brooder cum Mother Units.

schemes.name: Information, Monitoring, Evaluation and Social Audit (I-MESA): Central Smart Surveillance Unit (CSSU)
schemes.ministry: Ministry Of Social Justice and Empowerment
schemes.brief: The component aims to establish a Central Smart Surveillance Unit for continuous remote supervision of all Central Government-funded Grant-in-aid institutions and Training and Coaching centres using Closed-Circuit Television cameras and electronic devices.

schemes.name: Raw Material Assistance Scheme
schemes.ministry: Ministry Of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises
schemes.brief: Raw Material Assistance Scheme aims to help MSMEs by way of financing the purchase of Raw Material (Both indigenous & imported). This gives an opportunity to MSMEs to focus better on manufacturing quality products.

schemes.name: Agricultural Marketing Infrastructure
schemes.ministry: Ministry Of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare
schemes.brief: The revitalized and farmers’ income enhancing centric sub-scheme Agricultural Marketing Infrastructure (AMI) of Integrated Scheme for Agricultural Marketing (ISAM) is meant for holistic development of agricultural value chain critically focusing each linkage of post-harvest value chain.

schemes.name: Sardar Patel National Unity Award
schemes.brief: Under this scheme, the highest civilian recognition in the field of national unity is provided to individuals or institutions demonstrating exceptional dedication to strengthening the unity of India.

schemes.name: Ayush Oushadhi Gunvatta evam Uttpadan Samvardhan Yojana (AOGUSY): Support for Development of Standards and Accreditation/Certification of Ayush Products and Materials in Collaboration With BIS, QCI and Other Relevant Scientific Institutions and Industrial R&D Centres
schemes.brief: The scheme-component aims to support organizations and institutions in developing standards and accreditation frameworks for Ayush products and materials to ensure quality, safety, and scientific validation.

schemes.name: Ayush Oushadhi Gunvatta evam Uttpadan Samvardhan Yojana (AOGUSY): Strengthening and Up-Gradation of Ayush Pharmacies and Drug Testing Laboratories to Achieve Higher Standards
schemes.brief: The scheme-component aims to strengthen and upgrade ASU&H pharmacies and drug testing laboratories to improve quality control and manufacturing standards of Ayush drugs.

schemes.name: Jute Raw Material Bank Scheme
schemes.brief: Jute Raw Material Bank Scheme (JRMB) will be set up in the local clusters across the country in collaboration with bonafide Agencies who have adequate experience in similar activities and have the required storage, display, and selling infrastructure.

schemes.name: Coir Vikas Yojana- Coir Industry Technology Upgradation Scheme
schemes.ministry: Ministry Of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises
schemes.brief: The scheme “Coir Industry Technology Upgradation Scheme (CITUS)” is a component of “Coir Vikas Yojana” and the scheme was launched by Ministry of Micro, Small & Medium Enterprises, Government of India.

schemes.name: Production Linked Incentive Scheme (PLI) For Promoting Telecom & Networking Products Manufacturing In India
schemes.brief: Production Linked Incentive (PLI) Scheme aims to boost domestic manufacturing, investments and export in the telecom and networking products The PLI Scheme will be implemented within the overall financial limits of ₹ 12,195 Crores only (Rupees Twelve Thousand One Hundred and Ninety-Five Crore only)

schemes.name: Modified Market Development Assistance Scheme
schemes.ministry: Ministry Of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises
schemes.brief: The scheme aims to enhance the production and sales of Khadi and Polyvastra products by supporting technology upgradation, market promotion, and providing direct incentives to artisans and Karyakartas. It targets Khadi Institutions and their associated artisans and workers.

schemes.name: International Cooperation Scheme: Market Development Assistance
schemes.ministry: Ministry Of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises
schemes.brief: The scheme “International Cooperation Scheme” was launched by the MSME, Govt. of India with the aim to capacity build MSMEs for entering the export market by facilitating their participation in international exhibitions/fairs/conferences/seminars/buyer-seller meets abroad.

schemes.name: AICTE-Training programs for Physical Education Trainers/Sport-Games Teachers/Director of Sports
schemes.brief: Training programs for Physical Education Trainers/Sport-Games Teachers/Director of Sports, aims to support institution in conducting trainings to develop the skill of the participants to involve the masses in traditional sports/games and fun games for physical activity through recreation.

schemes.name: National Startup Awards
schemes.brief: The National Startup Awards by Startup India aims to recognize and reward outstanding startups and ecosystem enablers building innovative products or solutions and scalable enterprises.

schemes.name: Jute Resource-Cum-Production Centre
schemes.brief: Jute Resource-Cum-Production Centre (JRCPC) will be set up in the local clusters and localities across the country in collaboration with bonafide Agencies who have adequate experience in training and skilling programs and have required training cum-production infrastructure.

schemes.name: AICTE Grant for Organizing Conference
schemes.brief: The scheme provides financial assistance to institutions for organizing conferences in various fields of science and technology, management, and hotel management, including BBA, BCA and BMS institutions.

schemes.name: Export Promotion Capital Goods Scheme
schemes.brief: The objective of the EPCG Scheme is to facilitate import of capital goods for producing quality goods and services and enhance India’s manufacturing competitiveness. EPCG scheme allows import of capital goods for pre-production, production and post-production at zero customs duty.

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Jan Vikas Karyakram
schemes.brief: The Pradhan Mantri Jan Vikas Karyakram (PMJVK) is a Centrally Sponsored Scheme, being implemented with the objective to develop infrastructure projects such as community assets , in the identified areas with development deficits for socio-economic development of the said areas.

schemes.name: Loan Based Schemes For Safai Karamchari - Sanitary Marts Scheme
schemes.ministry: Ministry Of Social Justice and Empowerment
schemes.brief: A sanitary mart is a one-stop-shop for all things for sanitation and hygiene. It is a shopping place where the sanitary needs of the common man could be met. It serves both as a shop and as a service centre.

schemes.name: National Livestock Mission: Genetic Improvement of Pig breeds - Establishment of Pig Semen Collection and Processing Lab
schemes.ministry: Ministry of Fisheries,Animal Husbandry and Dairying
schemes.brief: The Scheme aims to enhance per-animal productivity, reduce inbreeding, and minimize sexually transmitted diseases in pigs. Through this scheme, financial assistance is provided for establishing semen processing labs at government pig farms.

schemes.name: National Handicrafts Development Programme: Research & Development Scheme
schemes.brief: The "NHDP: Research & Development Scheme" is introduced to generate feedback on economic, social, aesthetic, and promotional aspects of various crafts and artisans in the handicraft sector. The surveys and studies are sponsored/conducted to generate suitable inputs for planning purposes.

schemes.name: IVRI Institute Scholarship For M.V.Sc. And Ph.D.
schemes.ministry: Ministry Of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare
schemes.brief: The Indian Veterinary Research Institute will award scholarships to MVSc and PhD students who need financial assistance.his provision will be utilized to assist as many students as possible who have good academic record and who are making diligent efforts to pursue higher education.

schemes.name: Financial Assistance For Allied Cultural Activities
schemes.brief: The scheme provide financial assistance for creating infrastructure and assets that enhance audio-visual experiences for cultural activities, enabling regular live performances and festivals at locations that attract large numbers of tourists and visitors.

schemes.name: Mission on Integrated Development of Horticulture- NHM and HMNEH
schemes.ministry: Ministry Of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare
schemes.brief: The scheme aims to promote the holistic growth of the horticulture sector through production enhancement, productivity improvement, post-harvest management, processing, marketing support, skill development, and infrastructure creation for horticulture crops while augmenting farmers’ income.

schemes.name: Fund of Funds for Startups
schemes.brief: Launched in January 2016 under Startup India, the Fund of Funds for Startups is managed by SIDBI. It supports DPIIT-recognized startups indirectly by investing in SEBI-registered AIFs, which must invest at least twice the FFS contribution.

schemes.name: Scheme of Human Resources Development in Sports: Publication of Outstanding Works on Sports Related Subjects
schemes.brief: The scheme aims to provide financial assistance for the publication of outstanding works, journals, e-publications, online content, and web-based applications related to sports and games, thereby promoting sports knowledge, research, and awareness among stakeholders and the general public.

schemes.name: Khelo India Scheme: Fit India Movement
schemes.brief: The scheme aims to promote physical fitness among all citizens by encouraging regular physical activity, conducting fitness assessments, certifying schools, and organizing fitness campaigns and events.

schemes.name: Phase-II Of The Grid Connected Rooftop Solar Programme
schemes.ministry: Ministry Of New and Renewable Energy
schemes.brief: Phase-II of the Grid Connected Rooftop Solar Programme, this programme is introduced by Ministry of New & Renewable Energy(MNRE), GoI with JAKEDA as a nodal agency in the UT of J&K  for achieving cumulative capacity  of 220MW under 20MW  Residential Scheme and 200 MW Jammu Solar City “Solar City.

schemes.name: Scheme Of Assistance To Disabled Persons For Purchase/Fitting Of Aids/Appliances
schemes.ministry: Ministry Of Social Justice and Empowerment
schemes.brief: The main objective of the Scheme is to assist needy disabled persons in procuring durable, sophisticated, scientifically manufactured, modern, standard aids and appliances that can promote their physical, social and psychological rehabilitation by reducing the effect of disabilities.

schemes.name: Production Linked Incentive Scheme For Automobile And Auto Component Industry
schemes.brief: The government of India has launched the scheme “Production Linked Incentive (PLI) Scheme for Automobile and Auto Components Industry” in India to enhance India’s Manufacturing Capabilities for Advanced Automotive Products.

schemes.name: Scheme of Grant-in-Aid to Voluntary Organisations Working for the Welfare of Scheduled Tribes
schemes.brief: The Central Sector Scheme “Scheme of Grant-in-Aid to Voluntary Organisations Working for the Welfare of Scheduled Tribes” was launched by the Ministry of Tribal Affairs, Government of India.

schemes.name: Integrated Scheme on Agriculture Cooperation
schemes.ministry: Ministry Of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare
schemes.brief: The Integrated Scheme on Agriculture Cooperation (ISAC) is a Central Sector Scheme of the Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare, Government of India. It is being implemented through the National Cooperative Development Corporation (NCDC).

schemes.name: National Handloom Development Programme: Concessional Credit/Weaver MUDRA Scheme
schemes.brief: The scheme aims to provide adequate and timely assistance from the banks to the handloom sector to meet their credit requirements for term loan & working capital in a flexible and cost effective manner across the country.

schemes.name: Collaborative Research Scheme (CRS)
schemes.brief: Collaborative Research Scheme (CRS) has been introduced by the All India Council for Technical Education to support innovation & research across institutes with collaboration among TEQIP Faculty, Regular faculty of Project institutions (focus and non-focus) & Premier institutions of the country.

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Anusuchit Jaati Abhyuday Yojana (PM-AJAY) - Adarsh Gram
schemes.ministry: Ministry Of Social Justice and Empowerment
schemes.brief: The scheme aims to achieve integrated development of selected villages primarily through implementation of existing Central and State Government schemes in a convergent manner, ensuring access to quality services and infrastructure development in villages having SC population more than 40%.

schemes.name: Scheme of Development Assistance to Colleges for the Construction of Buildings
schemes.brief: The scheme aims to provide development assistance to Colleges to help in the consolidation and expansion of infrastructure. Financial assistance is provided to eligible Colleges for the construction and renovation of essential buildings like classrooms, laboratories, and hostels.

schemes.name: Ayurswasthya Yojana: AYUSH for Sports Medicine
schemes.brief: The scheme-component aims to promote AYUSH interventions for the treatment and rehabilitation of sports injuries and to enhance health and fitness among sports persons.

schemes.name: AICTE-PROFESSIONAL DEVELOPMENT SCHEME
schemes.brief: AICTE-Professional Development Scheme enables meritorious faculty to interact at International Level Conferences, both within and outside India, Seminars, and Symposia.

schemes.name: Electronic Manufacturing Clusters (EMC) Scheme: Greenfield Electronic Manufacturing Clusters
schemes.ministry: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology
schemes.brief: The aims to promote electronics manufacturing by providing financial assistance for cluster-based infrastructure. Through this scheme, infrastructure support is provided to electronics manufacturing units in identified clusters.

schemes.name: National Bamboo Mission
schemes.ministry: Ministry Of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare
schemes.brief: Restructured National Bamboo Mission was launched during 2018-19 as a Centrally Sponsored Scheme (CSS) after the historical amendment of the Indian Forest Act during 2017 to exclude bamboo out from definition of tree.

schemes.name: Ayush Oushadhi Gunvatta evam Uttpadan Samvardhan Yojana (AOGUSY): Strengthening of Central and State Regulatory Frameworks Including Technical Human Resource and Capacity Building Programs for Ayush Drugs
schemes.brief: The scheme component aims to strengthen the regulatory framework for Ayush drugs at the Central and State/UT levels by enhancing technical manpower, capacity building, quality control systems, and regulatory infrastructure.

schemes.name: Development Support for Coffee in Traditional Areas: Eco-certification of Coffee
schemes.brief: The objective of this scheme is to enable coffee growers to access high-value specialty markets and earn better returns by producing eco-certified coffee that meets established environmental and quality standards, ensuring transparency about the conditions under which the coffee is produced.

schemes.name: Production Linked Incentive (PLI) Scheme for Promotion of Domestic Manufacturing of Critical Key Starting Materials (KSMs)/ Drug Intermediates (DIs) and Active Pharmaceutical Ingredients (APIs) in India
schemes.brief: The objective of the Scheme is to attain self-reliance and reduce import dependence in critical KSMs/DIs/APIs. Under the Scheme, financial incentives shall be given based on threshold investment and domestic sales made by selected applicant for the eligible products.

schemes.name: Production Linked Incentive Scheme for Promoting Domestic Manufacturing of Medical Devices
schemes.brief: The objective of the Scheme is to provide financial incentive to boost domestic manufacturing and attract large investments in the Medical Device Sector.

schemes.name: Production Linked Incentive (PLI) Scheme for Textiles Part 2
schemes.brief: The scheme aims to promote MMF and technical textile production for global competitiveness and employment generation. Through this scheme, performance-based financial incentives are provided to textile manufacturers.

schemes.name: Scheme Of Financial Assistance For The Preservation And Development Of Cultural Heritage Of The Himalayas
schemes.brief: The scheme “Scheme of Financial Assistance for the Preservation and Development of Cultural Heritage of the Himalayas” was launched by the Ministry of Culture, Government of India.

schemes.name: Seva Bhoj Yojna
schemes.brief: The scheme “Seva Bhoj Yojna” was launched by the Ministry of Culture, Government of India and it has been made operational with effect from the 1st of August, 2018. The scheme shall be applicable within the territorial jurisdiction of India and will remain open from the 1st to 15th of every month.

schemes.name: Scheme of Fund for Regeneration of Traditional Industries
schemes.ministry: Ministry Of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises
schemes.brief: To make the traditional industries more productive and competitive and facilitating their sustainable development, the Govt. of India announced in 2005-06, setting up of a fund for the regeneration of traditional industries. Pursuant to this announcement, a Central Sector Scheme titled the “SFURTI".

schemes.name: Bank Loan Processing Fee Reimbursement Scheme
schemes.ministry: Ministry Of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises
schemes.brief: The Bank Loan Processing Fee Reimbursement Scheme comes under the purview of the Ministry of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (MSME) of the Government of India.

schemes.name: Reimbursement of membership Fee in Government promoted E-Commerce Portals
schemes.ministry: Ministry Of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises
schemes.brief: Reimbursement of Membership fee of Government promoted e-commerce portals Scheme is a sub scheme under National Scheduled Caste and Scheduled Tribe Hub (NSSH) scheme.

schemes.name: National Awards For Empowerment Of Persons With Disabilities: National Awards For Institutions Engaged In Empowering Persons With Disabilities: Sugamya Bharat Abhiyan Ke Karyanvayan/badhamukta Vatavaran Ke Srijan Mein Sarvshrestha Rajya/ Ut/ Zila
schemes.ministry: Ministry Of Social Justice and Empowerment
schemes.brief: The award is given by DoEPwD for outstanding work in Sugamya Bharat Abhiyan and the creation of a barrier-free environment/working conditions for persons with disabilities, the working place with easy mobility, toilet facilities, directions in sign language, etc.

schemes.name: E-YUVA Scheme: E-YUVA Centers
schemes.brief: E-YUVA Centers are housed within the University/Institute set-up. It acts as an anchor and extends requisite support and mentoring to students. Each EYC has a BIRAC-supported pre-incubation space which offers infrastructure and equipment for basic research and experimentation by selected students.

schemes.name: PM SETU (Pradhan Mantri Skilling and Employability Transformation through Upgraded ITIs) – Component II: Capacity Augmentation of Five National Skill Training Institutes (NSTIs) and Setting up of Five National Centres of Excellence for Skilling
schemes.ministry: Ministry Of Skill Development And Entrepreneurship
schemes.brief: The schemes aims to develop 5 NSTIs as premier National Centres of Excellence for Skilling. Through this scheme, infrastructure upgradation, new trades, and trainer development are provided to the 5 NSTIs and their stakeholders.

schemes.name: Personal Accident Insurance Scheme For Coir Workers
schemes.ministry: Ministry Of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises
schemes.brief: The scheme aims to provide financial compensation to deceased/disabled coir workers and their nominees. Through this scheme, insurance compensation benefits are provided to coir workers in case of accidents and disabilities.

schemes.name: Indian Community Welfare Fund
schemes.brief: A scheme by Ministry of External Affairs (MEA) for the welfare of Overseas Indians; assisting them in distress situations. ICWF has also been a critical support in emergency evacuation of Indian nationals from conflict zones, countries affected by natural disasters and other challenging situations.

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Gram Sadak Yojana
schemes.brief: With the objective of providing rural connectivity, the Government of India launched the Pradhan Mantri Gram Sadak Yojana (referred to as PMGSY-I hereinafter) on 25th December 2000 to provide all-weather access to eligible unconnected habitations.

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Jan Dhan Yojana
schemes.brief: A National Mission for Financial Inclusion (NMFI), namely, Pradhan Mantri Jan Dhan Yojana (PMJDY) was launched by the Prime Minister across the country on 28th August, 2014. It envisages to provide  universal banking services for every unbanked adult in the country.

schemes.name: Scheme For Campus Accommodation & Facilities Enhancing Social Experience
schemes.brief: "Scheme for Campus Accommodation & Facilities Enhancing Social Experience (CAFES)" was introduced by AICTE to provide financial assistance for construction of hostel to create a congenial study atmosphere free from the shackles of domestic shores so as to encourage students to purse their education.

schemes.name: AICTE - Scheme For Promoting Interests, Creativity And Ethics Among Students
schemes.brief: AICTE-SPICES scheme provides financial support to institutions for developing students' clubs for the well-rounded development of students by promoting their interests, creativity, and ethics. This club should serve as a model for other clubs in the institution and also those in other institutions.

schemes.name: Capacity Building For Service Providers - Skill Testing & Certification
schemes.brief: The scheme "Skill Testing & Certification" is a Suo-Moto Sub-Category of Programmes under the Umbrella Scheme of "Capacity Building for Service Providers", Ministry of Tourism. In this scheme, financial assistance is provided to conduct Skilling and Re-Skilling Programmes.

schemes.name: Single Point Registration Scheme
schemes.ministry: Ministry Of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises
schemes.brief: National Small Industries Corporation enlists Micro & Small Enterprises under Single Point Registration scheme for participation in Government Purchases. The units enlisted under this scheme of NSIC are eligible to get the benefits under Public Procurement Policy for Micro & Small Enterprises.

schemes.name: AICTE e-Shodh Sindhu Scheme (eSS)
schemes.brief:
AICTE e-Shodh Sindhu Scheme (eSS) provides subscriptions of e-Resources like Web of Science, Scopus,
ASME, ASCE and Bentham Science to institutions.

schemes.name: Pilgrimage Rejuvenation and Spiritual Augmentation Drive (PRASHAD)
schemes.brief: Launched in 2014–2015, the scheme "Pilgrimage Rejuvenation and Spiritual Augmentation Drive (PRASAD)" is a Central Sector Scheme by the Ministry of Tourism, Government of India. The scheme focuses on developing and identifying pilgrimage sites across India to enrich the religious tourism experience.

schemes.name: National Livestock Mission: Promotion of Piggery Entrepreneur
schemes.ministry: Ministry of Fisheries,Animal Husbandry and Dairying
schemes.brief: The scheme aims to promote entrepreneurship in pig farming by providing a one-time capital subsidy of up to ₹30 lakh to eligible entrepreneurs and organizations to establish breeder farms.

schemes.name: Shilp Guru Award
schemes.brief: The “Shilp Guru Award” for master craftspersons was introduced by the Ministry of Textiles to honor legendary master craftspersons of handicrafts whose work and dedication have contributed not only to the preservation but also to the promotion of the rich and diverse craft heritage of the country.

schemes.name: Scheme of Grants-in-Aid for supporting professional associations/ organisations of social scientists, companies and trusts duly registered under the Companies Act for their development
schemes.brief: The Indian Council of Social Science Research (ICSSR), under the Ministry of Education, Government of India, provides Grants-in-Aid for supporting professional associations/ organisations of social scientists, companies and trusts  for their development.

schemes.name: Ayurswasthya Yojana: AYUSH and Public Health
schemes.brief: The scheme-component aims to promote AYUSH-based interventions to improve community health outcomes by supporting preventive, promotive, curative, and rehabilitative healthcare services.

schemes.name: India BPO Promotion Scheme
schemes.ministry: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology
schemes.brief: The scheme aims to create employment opportunities and promote investment in the IT/ITeS sector by establishing BPO/ITeS units in smaller cities. Through this scheme, financial support in the form of Viability Gap Funding is provided to eligible companies.

schemes.name: Startup India Seed Fund Scheme- For Startups
schemes.brief: The scheme “Startup India Seed Fund Scheme (SISFS)” was launched by the DPIIT on 19th April 2021 with an outlay of INR 945 Crore to provide financial assistance to startups for Proof of Concept, prototype development, product trials, market-entry, and commercialization.

schemes.name: Electronic Manufacturing Clusters (EMC) Scheme: Common Facility Centres/ Brownfield Electronics Manufacturing Clusters
schemes.ministry: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology
schemes.brief: The scheme aims to support the upgradation of Brownfield EMCs through Common Facility Centres (CFCs). It provides financial assistance for infrastructure improvement and shared facilities for existing electronics manufacturing units.

schemes.name: Scheme for Promotion of Manufacturing of Electronic Components and Semiconductors (SPECS)"
schemes.ministry: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology
schemes.brief: The scheme aims to offset the disability for domestic manufacturing of components and semiconductors to strengthen the electronics manufacturing ecosystem. Through this scheme, a financial incentive of 25% on capital expenditure to legal entities registered in India.

schemes.name: Production Linked Incentive Scheme (PLI) for IT Hardware Scheme 2.O
schemes.ministry: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology
schemes.brief: The scheme aims to promote domestic manufacturing of Laptops, Tablets, All-in-One PCs, Servers, and Ultra Small Form Factor (USFF) devices in India through production-linked financial incentives.

schemes.name: Electronics Component Manufacturing Scheme
schemes.ministry: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology
schemes.brief: The scheme aims to provide financial incentives for manufacturing electronic components in Bharat. Through this scheme, turnover and capital expenditure-linked incentives are provided to companies and Limited Liability Partnerships.

schemes.name: Sagar Samman Award- Sagar Samman Varuna Award
schemes.brief: This scheme aims to recognize outstanding maritime contributions by providing top national recognition to distinguished maritime professionals.

schemes.name: Ayush Oushadhi Gunvatta evam Uttpadan Samvardhan Yojana (AOGUSY): Pharmacovigilance of ASU&H Drugs Including Surveillance of Misleading Advertisements
schemes.brief: The scheme-component aims to establish a robust safety monitoring and surveillance system for Ayurveda, Siddha, Unani and Homoeopathy (ASU&H) drugs and to monitor misleading advertisements related to Ayush medicines.

schemes.name: Assistance to Training Institutions Scheme
schemes.ministry: Ministry Of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises
schemes.brief: The scheme aims to provide financial assistance for creation, strengthening, and expansion of training institutions under MSME for skill development and entrepreneurship training.

schemes.name: Development Support for Coffee in Traditional Areas: Support to Small Growers' Collectives/ SHGs/ Cooperatives for Coffee Marketing
schemes.brief: The objective of the scheme is to extend support to small and tiny growers, incentivizing them to form collectives/ Self Help Groups/ Cooperatives, thus encouraging them to take up marketing of coffee produced on a community-based approach through suitable financial incentives.

schemes.name: Shahari Waqf Sampatti Vikas Yojana
schemes.brief: Under the scheme, interest free loan to Waqf Boards (WBs)/waqf institutions is granted to various Waqf Boards and Waqf Institutions in the country for construction of economically viable buildings on the Waqf land, such as commercial complex, marriage halls, hospitals, cold storage etc..

schemes.name: Comprehensive Handicrafts Cluster Development Scheme
schemes.brief: The scheme aims to provide financial assistance, infrastructure development, skill upgradation, and market linkage support to artisans, craftsmen, and cluster-based enterprises.

schemes.name: Consortia & Tender Marketing Scheme
schemes.ministry: Ministry Of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises
schemes.brief: The scheme aims to facilitate Micro and Small Enterprises (MSEs) in marketing their products and services through individual or collective participation in tenders via consortia. It helps MSEs secure bulk orders, improve competitiveness, and access government procurement opportunities.

schemes.name: Technology Development And Utilization Programme For Women
schemes.brief: In order to meet the specific needs of women and to enhance the contribution of women towards technology capability building, the “Technology Development & Utilization Programme for Women (TDUPW)” has been formulated by the Department of Scientific and Industrial Research (DSIR).

schemes.name: Flexible Memorandum of Understanding (Flexi-MoU) Scheme
schemes.ministry: Ministry Of Skill Development And Entrepreneurship
schemes.brief: The scheme is designed to cater to the needs of both industry as well as trainees. The scheme allows industries to train candidates as per their skill set requirements and provides trainees with an industry environment aligned with the market demand and latest technology to undergo training.

schemes.name: Coir Vikas Yojana- Export Market Promotion Scheme
schemes.ministry: Ministry Of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises
schemes.brief: Coir Board is implementing the Central Sector Scheme “Export Market Promotion Scheme” with a view to improve the export performance of Indian Coir Sector through various export market promotion activities.

schemes.name: Production Linked Incentive Scheme Tranche-I under National Programme on High Efficiency Solar PV Modules
schemes.ministry: Ministry Of New and Renewable Energy
schemes.brief: The scheme aims to promote the manufacturing of high-efficiency solar photovoltaic modules and reduce import dependence. Through this scheme, Production Linked Incentive is provided to eligible manufacturers.

schemes.name: MSME Sustainable (ZED) Certification Scheme
schemes.ministry: Ministry Of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises
schemes.brief: MSME Sustainable (ZED) Certification Scheme is an extensive drive to create awareness amongst MSMEs about Zero Defect Zero Effect (ZED) practices and motivate and incentivize them for ZED Cerficaon while also encouraging them to become MSME Champions.

schemes.name: VAIshwik BHArtiya Vaigyanik (VAIBHAV) Fellowship
schemes.brief:
VAIBHAV Fellowship envisages collaboration between scientists of Indian Diaspora with
Indian Higher Educational Institutions/Universities/Public Funded Scientific
Institutions. Fellow would identify an Indian Institution for collaboration &
may spend upto two months in a year for a max. of 3 years.

schemes.name: Scheme for Grant of Financial Assistance for Administering Road Safety Advocacy and Awards for the Outstanding Work Done in the Field of Road Safety
schemes.brief: The objective of the Scheme is to stimulate and encourage opportunities for various agencies through participation, in order to create awareness on road safety.

schemes.name: Intellectual Property Right Scheme Under MSME Innovative Scheme
schemes.ministry: Ministry Of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises
schemes.brief: Intellectual Property Right Scheme offers legal and intellectual property filing support including patents, trademarks, copyrights, designs, geographical indications (GI) etc.

schemes.name: Export Promotion Council Membership Reimbursement Scheme for Scheduled Caste/Scheduled Tribe
schemes.ministry: Ministry Of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises
schemes.brief: Export Promotion Council Membership Reimbursement Scheme provides  reimbursement to an SC/ST Micro & small enterprises(MSEs), on annual membership subscription fee / one-time subscription charges/entry fee charged by various Export Promotion Councils (EPC).

schemes.name: AICTE-IDEA (Idea Development, Evaluation & Application) LAB SCHEME
schemes.brief: Through Idea Lab Scheme, AICTE will be setting up Idea Labs in AICTE approved institutions to encourage students for application of science, technology engineering and mathematics (STEM) fundamentals towards enhanced hands-on experience, learning by doing and even product visualization.

schemes.name: Scheme for Setting Up of Plastic Parks
schemes.brief: The scheme aims to increase the competitiveness and value addition in the plastic processing industry through cluster development. It provides grant-in-aid up to ₹40,00,00,000/- for common infrastructure benefits intended for Special Purpose Vehicles and local enterprises.

schemes.name: Star College Scheme For Strengthening Of Science Education And Training At Undergraduate Level
schemes.brief: The Dept. of Biotechnology has launched "Star College Scheme for Strengthening of Science Education and Training at Undergraduate Level" for improving critical thinking and 'hands-on' experimental work at the undergraduate (college) level in sciences.

schemes.name: Supporting Community Radio Movement in India
schemes.brief: The GOI has a policy for grant of License to eligible organisations for setting up of Community Radio Stations. To support the Sector, the Scheme provides financial assistance once the Community Radio is made operational by eligible organisation. The Scheme also has components for capacity building.

schemes.name: Production Linked Incentive (PLI) Scheme for Automobile and Auto Components Industry
schemes.brief: The scheme offers financial incentives to boost domestic manufacturing of Advanced Automotive Technology products and attract investments in the automotive manufacturing value chain.

schemes.name: Infrastructure and Technology Support
schemes.brief: The sub-scheme “Infrastructure and Technology Support” aims to develop world-class infrastructure in the country to support handicraft production, enhance product quality, and reduce costs to enable it to compete in the global market.

schemes.name: Advance Authorisation(AA)
schemes.brief: Advance Authorisation scheme enables duty free import of inputs for export production, including replenishment of inputs or duty remission Advance Authorisation covers manufacturer exporters or merchant exporters tied to supporting manufacturer(s).

schemes.name: Financial Assistance For Development Of Buddhist/Tibetan Arts And Culture
schemes.brief: The scheme “Financial Assistance for Development of Buddhist/Tibetan Arts and Culture” was launched by the Ministry of Culture, Government of India to provide financial assistance to voluntary Buddhist/Tibetan organizations including Monasteries.

schemes.name: Loan Based Schemes For Safai Karamchari - Swachhta Udyami Yojana – Swachhta Se Sampannta Ki Aur
schemes.ministry: Ministry Of Social Justice and Empowerment
schemes.brief: The scheme aims to ensure mechanised cleaning and provide related safe and sustainable livelihood opportunities to Safai Karamcharis and liberated Manual Scavengers. The Financial assistance is provided for the procurement and operation of sanitation-related equipment and vehicles for the target.

schemes.name: Cultural Function And Production Grant Scheme
schemes.brief: The scheme covers all ‘not-for-profit’ organizations, NGOs, Societies, Trusts, and Universities for supporting Seminars, Conference, Research, Workshops, Festivals, Exhibitions, Symposia, Drama-Theatre, Music, etc., and small research projects on different aspects of Indian Culture.

schemes.name: Credit Based Schemes For SC - Term Loan (TL)
schemes.ministry: Ministry Of Social Justice and Empowerment
schemes.brief: The scheme aims to provide financial assistance for viable income-generating activities of Scheduled Caste beneficiaries. Through this scheme, term loans of up to 95% of the project cost are provided for projects costing up to ₹50,00,000/-.

schemes.name: Khelo India Scheme: Khelo India Centres and Sports Academies
schemes.brief: The scheme aims to encourage sports across the country by strengthening sports infrastructure, coaching, talent development, and sports academies, thereby promoting excellence in sports and holistic development of children and youth.

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Formalisation Of Micro Food Processing Enterprises
schemes.brief: Launched on 29th June 2020, PMFMPE is a Centrally Sponsored Scheme by the MoFPI, designed to address the challenges faced by the micro enterprises and to tap the potential of groups and cooperatives in supporting the upgradation and formalization of these enterprises.

schemes.name: Modernisation And Removal Of Obsolescence
schemes.brief: MODROB scheme, one of the AICTE Quality Improvement Scheme (AQIS), intends to support the development of laboratories with appropriate equipment/ technology/ tools/ software and encourages financial contribution from the industry.

schemes.name: Sub-mission On Agriculture Mechanization
schemes.ministry: Ministry Of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare
schemes.brief: The Sub-Mission on Agricultural Mechanization was launched in 2014-15 by the Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers’ Welfare.

schemes.name: Galvanizing Organic Bio-Agro Resources Dhan (GOBARdhan)
schemes.brief: GOBARdhan Scheme was launched in April 2018 as a part of the Solid and Liquid Waste Management component under Swachh Bharat Mission (Grameen) to positively impact village cleanliness and generate wealth and energy from cattle and organic waste. The main focus of GOBARDHAN is to keep villages clean.

schemes.name: National Handicrafts Development Programme: Infrastructure And Technology Support: URBAN HAAT
schemes.brief: The scheme "Infrastructure & Technology Support: URBAN HAAT" is introduced is to set up a permanent marketing infrastructure in towns/ metropolitan cities to provide direct marketing facilities to handicrafts artisans/handloom weavers, enabling them to sell their products to wider target audience.

schemes.name: National Awards For Empowerment Of Persons With Disabilities: National Awards For Institutions Engaged In Empowering Persons With Disabilities: Divyangjano Ke Liye Sarvshrestha Niyoktha
schemes.ministry: Ministry Of Social Justice and Empowerment
schemes.brief:
The "Divyangjano Ke Liye Sarvshrestha Niyoktha" award is given by DoEPwD to the outstanding employers of the disabled under: 
1.  Government organization.
2.  Public Sector Undertaking or Autonomous Body or Local Government Body.
3.  Private or Non-Governmental Organization.

schemes.name: National Awards For Empowerment Of Persons With Disabilities: National Awards For Institutions Engaged In Empowering Persons With Disabilities: Divyangjanon Ke Sashaktikaran Men Karyrat Sarveshrestha Sansthan (Private Organization, NGO)
schemes.ministry: Ministry Of Social Justice and Empowerment
schemes.brief: The "Divangjano ke Shashaktikaran Mein Karyrat Sarveshrestha Sansthan" award is given by DoEPwD to an organization working for the empowerment of Persons with Disabilities for a minimum period of 10 years after registration under Society Act/ Trust Act / Section 8 of Companies Act 2013.

schemes.name: Financial Assistance For Cultural Activities In Performing Arts For Building Grants Including Studio Theatres
schemes.brief: The scheme “Financial Assistance for Cultural Activities in Performing Arts for Building Grants Including Studio Theatres” was launched by the Ministry of Culture, Government of India.

schemes.name: Agriculture And Processed Foods Export Promotion Scheme Of APEDA For The 15th Finance Commission Cycle (2021-22 To 2025-26)
schemes.brief: The Financial Assistance Scheme (FAS) is an export promotion scheme run by APEDA. The scheme aims to facilitate the export of agri-products by providing assistance to exporters.

schemes.name: Tea Development and Promotion Scheme (TDPS): Plantation Development for Small Tea Growers (PDSTG): Mechanization for Individual Small Growers
schemes.brief: The objective of this component is to increase the production, productivity and quality of tea, overall development of small tea growers to help them move up in the value chain, sustainability of production by promoting organic tea cultivation& climate resilient tea and to make them self-reliant.

schemes.name: National Livestock Mission: Establishment of State Semen Bank
schemes.ministry: Ministry of Fisheries,Animal Husbandry and Dairying
schemes.brief: "Establishment of State Semen Bank (Genetic Improvement of Sheep and Goat breeds)" aims at the genetic improvement of sheep/goat breeds through selective breeding by propagation of superior male germplasm through artificial insemination.

schemes.name: Scheme of Financial Assistance to IHMs/FCIs/IITTM/ICI/NCHMCT/PSUs
schemes.brief: The scheme aims to provide Central Financial Assistance for setting up, expanding, upgrading, and replacing equipment for institutions involved in tourism and hospitality education and capacity building. Benefits include capital grants for construction and equipment.

schemes.name: Bharat Steel Awards
schemes.brief: The scheme aims to recognise excellence and outstanding contributions in the Indian steel sector. Through this scheme, awards and incentives are provided to eligible steel companies and individuals.

schemes.name: Ayush Oushadhi Gunvatta Evam Uttpadan Samvardhan Yojana
schemes.brief: The scheme aims to enhance India's manufacturing capabilities and exports of traditional medicines and health promotion products under the initiative of Atmanirbhar Bharat.

schemes.name: Ayurswasthya Yojana: Up-gradation of Facilities to Centre of Excellence (CoE)
schemes.brief: The scheme-component aims to support the establishment and up-gradation of reputed institutions to Centres of Excellence for strengthening AYUSH education, research, innovation, and advanced healthcare services.

schemes.name: Scheme of Grant-in-Aid to Voluntary and other Organizations Working for Scheduled Castes
schemes.ministry: Ministry Of Social Justice and Empowerment
schemes.brief: The "Scheme aims to enhance the reach of development interventions of the Government and fill the gaps in service-deficient Scheduled Caste-dominant areas in sectors such as education, health, vocational training, etc., through the efforts of voluntary organizations and other organizations.

schemes.name: National Action for Mechanised Sanitation Ecosystem (NAMASTE) - "Emergency Response Sanitation Unit (ERSU) Formation and Functionalisation"
schemes.ministry: Ministry Of Social Justice and Empowerment
schemes.brief: The scheme aims to establish professional, well-trained, motivated and adequately equipped workforce for maintenance and management of sewers and septic tanks, addressing the critical need to eliminate fatalities resulting from workers entering sewers and septic tanks without proper safety measures.

schemes.name: Startup India Seed Fund Scheme- For Incubators
schemes.brief: The scheme “Startup India Seed Fund Scheme (SISFS)” was launched by the DPIIT on 19th April 2021 to provide financial assistance to startups for proof of concept, prototype development, product trials, market-entry, and commercialization.

schemes.name: Scheme for Reimbursement of Testing and Certification Charges for Start-ups and Telecom MSEs
schemes.ministry: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology
schemes.brief: The scheme aims to reimburse testing and certification costs. Financial support is provided to Start-ups and MSEs to enhance telecom product competitiveness.

schemes.name: Production Linked Incentive Scheme (PLI) for IT Hardware
schemes.ministry: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology
schemes.brief: The scheme aims to boost domestic manufacturing and attract investment in IT hardware. Through this scheme, financial incentives are provided to eligible manufacturers based on incremental sales.

schemes.name: Livestock Health and Diseases Control
schemes.ministry: Ministry of Fisheries,Animal Husbandry and Dairying
schemes.brief: The Livestock Health and Diseases Control (LH&DC) scheme is a Centrally Sponsored Scheme (CSS) implemented by the Department of Animal Husbandry and Dairying (DAHD), Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers' Welfare, Government of India.

schemes.name: Amended Technology Upgradation Fund
schemes.brief: The Ministry of Textiles introduced the Amended Technology Upgradation Fund Scheme (ATUFS). This scheme aims to facilitate investment, employment, productivity, quality and import and export substitution in the textile industry.
