schemes.name: National Awards for e-Governance
schemes.ministry: Ministry Of Personnel, Public Grievances And Pensions
schemes.brief: The scheme aims to recognize excellence in e-governance. It provides awards and incentives up to ₹10,00,000 to outstanding initiatives.

schemes.name: Subhash Chandra Bose Aapda Prabandhan Puraskar
schemes.brief: The scheme aims to recognize excellent work done by individuals and institutions in the field of disaster management including prevention, mitigation, preparedness, response, and rehabilitation.

schemes.name: Financial Assistance For Promotion Of Guru-Shishya Parampara (Repertory Grant)
schemes.brief: This scheme provides financial support to organizations preserving traditional performing arts through the Guru–Shishya tradition and ensures continuity of cultural heritage through training and mentorship.

schemes.name: Coffee Development Programme in North Eastern Region: Support for Group Nurseries
schemes.brief: The scheme "Coffee Development Programme in North Eastern Region: Support for Group Nurseries" by the Coffee Board, Department of Commerce, Ministry of Commerce and Industry, aims to provide growers with high-quality coffee seedlings to ensure the proper establishment of plantations.

schemes.name: Access to Knowledge for Technology Development and Dissemination (A2K+)
schemes.brief: Access to Knowledge for Technology Development and Dissemination (A2K+) is a scheme targeted towards developing mechanisms to disseminate science, technology and innovation-related information to industries, research and academic institutions

schemes.name: Production Linked Incentive (PLI) Scheme for Food Processing Industry
schemes.brief: The scheme aims to promote food manufacturing, branding, and employment. Through this scheme, financial incentives and branding support are provided to food processing entities.

schemes.name: Production Linked Incentive Scheme for Millet Based Products
schemes.brief: The scheme aims to increase usage of millets and promote value-added millet products. Through this scheme, financial incentives on incremental sales are provided to eligible manufacturers.

schemes.name: International Cooperation Scheme- Capacity Building of First Time MSE Exporters
schemes.ministry: Ministry Of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises
schemes.brief: The scheme “International Cooperation Scheme” was launched by the MSME, Govt. of India with the aim to capacity build MSMEs for entering export market by providing reimbursement of various costs incurred by first time MSE exporters on export shipments.

schemes.name: Mission Solar Charkha
schemes.ministry: Ministry Of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises
schemes.brief: The scheme “Mission Solar Charkha” a pilot project was implemented by the Ministry of Micro, Small & Medium Enterprises, Government of India. The scheme envisages setting up of Solar Charkha Clusters which would mean a focal village and other surrounding villages in a radius of 8 to 10 Kilometres.

schemes.name: Design Scheme Under MSME Innovative Scheme
schemes.ministry: Ministry Of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises
schemes.brief: Design scheme will help MSMEs to avail advice on all aspects of design. It helps MSMEs realize and achieve their design-related objectives. This specialist advice will be provided by experienced designers for new product development as well as enhancing existing product portfolio.

schemes.name: Deen Dayal Disabled Rehabilitation Scheme
schemes.ministry: Ministry Of Social Justice and Empowerment
schemes.brief: The umbrella Central Sector Scheme of this Ministry called the "Scheme to Promote Voluntary Action for Persons with Disabilities" was revised w.e.f. 01.04.2003 and was renamed as the "Deendayal Disabled Rehabilitation Scheme (DDRS)"

schemes.name: Prime Minister's Employment Generation Programme
schemes.ministry: Ministry Of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises
schemes.brief: Launched in August 2008, Prime Minister's Employment Generation Programme (PMEGP) is a credit-linked subsidy scheme, administered by the Ministry of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (MSME). PMEGP aims to generate employment opportunities through the establishment of micro-enterprises in

schemes.name: Industrial R&D Promotion Programme: Recognition Of In-House R&D Units
schemes.brief: DSIR is operating a scheme called "Recognition of In-House R&D Units" with the objective of granting recognition & registration to in-house R&D units established by the corporate industry. The In-house R&D units are expected to be engaged in innovative research & development activities.

schemes.name: Institutional Support for Development and Marketing of Tribal Products/Produce
schemes.brief: The scheme “Institutional Support for development and Marketing of Tribal Products/Produce” was introduced in 2014-15 by the Ministry of Tribal Affairs, Government of India.

schemes.name: National Livestock Mission: Genetic Improvement of Pig Breeds - Import of Exotic Pig Germplasm
schemes.ministry: Ministry of Fisheries,Animal Husbandry and Dairying
schemes.brief: The scheme aims to enhance indigenous pig productivity through genetic upgradation. The scheme supports the establishment of pig semen collection and processing labs and the import of exotic pig germplasm.

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Skilling and Employability Transformation through Upgraded ITIs (PM‑SETU) : Component I
schemes.ministry: Ministry Of Skill Development And Entrepreneurship
schemes.brief: The scheme aims to upgrade ITIs into industry-led institutions. Through this scheme, infrastructure, training, and employment-oriented benefits are provided to trainees.

schemes.name: Vanchit Ikai Samooh aur Vargon ki Aarthik Sahayata Yojana (VISVAS) for Self-Help Groups
schemes.ministry: Ministry Of Social Justice and Empowerment
schemes.brief: The scheme aims to provide the benefit of a lower rate of interest to eligible Self-Help Groups (SHGs) and individual beneficiaries belonging to Scheduled Castes (SCs), Other Backward Classes (OBCs), and Safai Karamcharis (including identified Manual Scavengers, Waste Pickers, and their dependents).

schemes.name: Credit Guarantee Scheme for Startups
schemes.brief: Under this scheme to improve access to collateral-free debt funding for DPIIT-recognised startups by providing credit guarantees on eligible loan and debt facilities, thereby encouraging institutional lending and supporting startup growth and innovation.

schemes.name: Maulana Abul Kalam Azad Trophy Scheme
schemes.brief: The MAKA Trophy instituted in year 1956-57 is a rolling trophy awarded by the Ministry of Sports to the overall top performing University in sports of the preceding year for promoting competitive sports in the university sector with a view to arouse keen interest among students.

schemes.name: National Mission on Edible Oils- Oil Palm
schemes.ministry: Ministry Of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare
schemes.brief: The Mission was launched with the aim to enhance the edible oilseeds production and oils availability in the country by harnessing Oil Palm area expansion, increasing CPO production and to reduce import burden on edible oils.

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Kaushal Vikas Yojana 4.0- Special Projects
schemes.ministry: Ministry Of Skill Development And Entrepreneurship
schemes.brief: The scheme aims to address the skilling needs of marginalised groups and difficult geographies. Project-based short-term skill training is provided to such candidates.

schemes.name: Khelo India Scheme: Promotion of Inclusiveness through Sports
schemes.brief: The scheme aims to promote social inclusion, gender equality, peace, development, and participation in sports by supporting rural and indigenous games, sports for women, persons with disabilities, and youth in disturbed areas across the country.

schemes.name: Capacity Building For Service Providers - Entrepreneurship Programme
schemes.brief: The scheme "Entrepreneurship Programme" is a Suo-Moto Sub-Scheme of the Ministry of Tourism under the Umbrella Scheme of "Capacity Building for Service Providers". In this scheme financial assistance is provided to the institutes (given below) to conduct entrepreneurship development programs.

schemes.name: Kisan Vikas Patra Scheme
schemes.brief: ‘Kisan Vikas Patra Scheme’ was relaunched in the year 2014 by the Department of Economic Affairs, Ministry Of Finance, Government of India in view of the popular demand and to revitalize Small Savings. The amount invested in Kisan Vikas Patra (KYP) doubles in 115 months at the present rate.

schemes.name: Loan based Scheme for Pay and Use Community Toilets
schemes.ministry: Ministry Of Social Justice and Empowerment
schemes.brief: The “Pay and Use” Community Toilets Scheme under Swachhta Udyami Yojana by NSKFDC offers concessional loans to Safai Karamcharis and their dependents for setting up public toilet units, promoting sanitation, livelihood, and dignity.

schemes.name: GROUP WORKSHED SCHEME
schemes.brief: The objective of the scheme is to facilitate the establishment of worksheds for shuttleless looms in an existing or new cluster, which will provide the required scale of economy for business operations.

schemes.name: Bima Yojana - Direct Benefit to Artisan
schemes.brief: Launched on 15th August 2022, the scheme "Bima Yojana - Direct Benefit to Artisan (BYDBTA)" is a Social Welfare Scheme by the Ministry of Textiles, Government of India.

schemes.name: National Centre for Communication Security (NCCS) Research Associates Scheme
schemes.brief: NCCS engages young scholars as Research Associates  to contribute in development of Telecom Security eco system with in India by providing an opportunity to them to work in areas like Telecom Security Standard development, Lab recognition and Security Certification of telecom products.
